description: SAS -> PySpark translation complexity. Extends the Spark SQL profile and restates only the constructs that rate differently when a full Python host language surrounds the DataFrame API. Tiers are unchanged throughout — the data-complexity of a SAS construct does not depend on what it is being translated into; only the parity ratings move.

MACRO_DEFINITION.note: %MACRO definition -> a parameterised Python function; a real mapping exists here, unlike in pure SQL, but the code-generation semantics still have to be unwound

MACRO_CONTROL_FLOW.note: macro-level control flow -> ordinary Python if/for around DataFrame calls

MACRO_CALL.note: macro invocation -> a Python function call

do_loop.note: iterative DO -> a Python loop over columns, or a pandas UDF; still a rewrite, but expressible in the host language

do_while.note: DO WHILE -> unbounded row-wise iteration; a pandas UDF can express it, at a cost

do_until.note: DO UNTIL -> unbounded row-wise iteration; a pandas UDF can express it, at a cost

array.note: ARRAY still aliases a group of columns, not a Spark ArrayType — but a Python loop over df.columns expresses the wide-to-long rewrite directly

execute.note: CALL EXECUTE generates code at run time; Python can build and run the equivalent, so this is a rewrite rather than a redesign

module.note: external routine call -> a Python library call, where an equivalent library exists

symget.note: runtime macro-variable read -> a Python variable, once the macro layer is unwound

resolve.note: resolves macro code at run time -> Python string building plus an explicit call

dosubl.note: submits SAS code mid-step -> a Python function call at the same point

fcmp.note: PROC FCMP defines custom functions -> a Python function, registered as a UDF where it is used in an expression

flags.name: defines-macro
flags.note: defines a macro -> a parameterised Python function

flags.name: invokes-macro
flags.note: invokes a macro -> a Python function call
